line_preview: Title: Learning Curve: Progress in the Replication Crisis
Authors: NOAM ANGRIST, CLAIRE CULLEN, MICHEAL AINOMUGISHA, SAI PRAMOD BATHENA, PETER
BERGMAN, COLIN CROSSLEY, THATO LETSOMO, MOITSHEPI MATSHENG, RENE MARLON PANTI,
global main "/Users/sant4625/Dropbox/R&I/22_Lowtech Education Replication Trials/_ConnectEd Implementation Paper/Program_Files"
global main "/Users/bsog0523/Dropbox/22_Lowtech Education Replication Trials/_ConnectEd Implementation Paper/Program_Files"
/* Replace `main' file path with current working directory, pointing to the downloaded repository.
* FIGURE 1. LEARNING CURVE – TARGETED INSTRUCTION ACROSS STUDIES
replace trial_order =3 if trial_order ==5 /*Philippines govt. and NGO arm grouped together for this trial-by-trial analysis*/
Benchmark rates in the first data point are derived from studies in Bihar
and Uttar Pradesh, India from the non-targeted treatment groups. Rates
are the weighted average of the percentage of time classes were
grouped by ability level in Table 2 in Banerjee et al. (2017). */
*accurately_targeted is defined as accurate targeting instruction for comparable weeks based on monitoring data on a student's true level and the level they were subsequently taught.
